published_prior_art.cite: Rosenbaum1987SensitivityPermutation and Rosenbaum1988SensitivityMultipleControls give statistic-specific sharp sensitivity analyses; HuberStrassen1973Minimax supplies the representative-pair framework.

HuberStrassen1973Minimax supplies general least-favourable-distribution machinery; CohenOlsonFogarty2020AdversarialGame, Rosenbaum1988SensitivityMultipleControls, GastwirthKriegerRosenbaum2000Separability, and Fogarty2025Tilted address related but statistic-restricted matched-study problems.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 / related work
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey omits Rosenbaum1988SensitivityMultipleControls, the direct finite multiple-control Rosenbaum sensitivity paper; distinguish its selected signed-rank family from the proposed all-tests game.

soundness_flags.label: def:causal-bayes-factor and def:alternative-law
soundness_flags.one_line: The Radon--Nikodym scores require stated domination, positive measurable density versions, and common support; otherwise Q_i(j;r_i) at a conditioned record is not defined invariantly.

soundness_flags.label: def:alternative-law; thm:pair-lf-frontier; oeq:set-mixture-frontier
soundness_flags.one_line: Bayes' rule from F_{1ij}/F_{0i} gives a conditional assignment law proportional to the alternative assignment prior times Q_i(j;r_i), not Q_i(j;r_i) alone; no alternative assignment prior or independence condition is supplied, so P_{1,n}^Q is not induced by the claimed causal alternative.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: pair theorem and matched-set mixture question
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The claims are conditional on a Fisher-sharp-null matched-study game with fixed Gamma and a specified causal outcome alternative, not weak-null or ATE inference.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Exact least-favourable-law statements apply only after a coherent alternative assignment mechanism and conditional outcome likelihood model are specified.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add an alternative assignment baseline pi_i(j), domination/common-support assumptions, and derive P_1(Z=z|R=r) proportional to product_i pi_i(z_i)Q_i(z_i;r_i); then state whether pi is fixed, uniform, or adversarially coupled to the Gamma-null.
Add Rosenbaum (1988), Sensitivity analysis for matching with multiple controls, and give a precise boundary between its selected-statistic family and the all-tests finite saddle-point target.

Promote a worked n=1,m=3 saddle-point calculation with explicit mixture weights and support-switch inequalities to the first main result, then state the general product-set characterization as the extension problem.

recommended_next_step: Revise the causal Bayes-to-assignment-law link and add the omitted multiple-control comparator before advancing; the repaired finite all-tests frontier remains potentially field-tier.
